APAC.assessment: APAC has maintained continuous escalation across all seven observed pipeline runs, with severity trending upward toward Critical in the most recent cycles. State-aligned pressure on regional manufacturing infrastructure has intensified, with the Kaohsiung production hub carrying the highest concentration of exposure. The trajectory indicates a sustained and worsening threat environment rather than an isolated incident — the business risk is structural, not episodic.

AME.assessment: AME has been escalated at Critical severity in six of seven pipeline runs, with Ransomware as the consistent primary scenario across the entire observation window. The Houston service hub and North American logistics network remain the primary exposure points. The threat posture is entrenched rather than escalating — the $22M VaCR exposure has been a persistent fixture of every board cycle since monitoring began.

LATAM.assessment: LATAM has remained below escalation threshold across all seven pipeline runs, with one brief elevation to Medium in the fifth cycle that did not persist. The regional service and maintenance presence in Brazil and Chile continues to operate in a stable threat environment. No primary scenario has been identified — the region represents a low-risk operating context.

MED.assessment: MED has been escalated in five of seven runs, primarily driven by insider misuse indicators linked to the Palermo manufacturing site and regional political instability across the Southern European corridor. Severity has been predominantly Medium with one High event, suggesting a chronic low-level exposure rather than an acute threat. The $4.2M VaCR exposure reflects a contained but persistent risk that has shown no signs of resolution across the observation window.

NCE.assessment: NCE has cleared all seven pipeline runs without escalation. The Hamburg and Gdansk manufacturing hubs and the Copenhagen service operation continue to operate in the lowest observed threat environment across all five regions. The region's stable posture has been consistent throughout the entire observation window.

patterns: AME and APAC have both been escalated in every observed pipeline run — the two highest-VaCR regions are simultaneously under persistent pressure.
No region has transitioned from escalated to clear within the observation window, suggesting structural rather than transient threat conditions.
MED insider misuse indicators have appeared independently of the state-aligned pressures driving APAC and AME escalations.
cross_regional.compound_risk: AeroGrid carries simultaneous persistent escalation in its two largest exposure regions — AME ($22M VaCR, Ransomware) and APAC ($18.5M VaCR, System intrusion). Combined, these represent $40.5M in quantified exposure that has been continuously flagged across all seven observed cycles. The compound risk is not additive in isolation — a simultaneous disruption event across both regions would place manufacturing continuity and service delivery under concurrent strain with no clear backstop region.

ciso_talking_points: AME ransomware exposure of $22M has been flagged at Critical severity in six of the last seven pipeline runs — this is not an emerging threat, it is an entrenched posture.
APAC severity has trended upward to Critical in the most recent two cycles, suggesting the threat environment is deteriorating rather than stabilising.
Combined AME and APAC VaCR exposure stands at $40.5M — both regions have been simultaneously escalated in every observed pipeline cycle.
MED insider misuse indicators have persisted across five of seven runs at the Palermo site, representing a chronic low-level exposure the business has not yet resolved.
LATAM and NCE remain clear across all observed runs and provide no evidence of spillover from the pressures affecting the other three regions.
